session.assistant:
Now let me also clean up the PR-already-exists test. The approach of doing a first dispatch to discover the branch name is overly complex. Since I'm using `slug="fix-config-import"`, the branch name is deterministic (it uses `generate_planned_pr_branch_name` which includes a timestamp from `ctx.time`). But since the FakeGitHub `get_pr_for_branch` checks by exact branch name, I need to know the generated branch name. Let me think about a better approach.
Actually, the simplest approach: I can use `prs_by_branch` with a callback-style approach, but the fake just uses a dict lookup. The challenge is that the branch name includes a timestamp. However, the `FakeGitHub.get_pr_for_branch` iterates `_prs_by_branch`. Since I don't know the exact branch name ahead of time, I should test via the `dispatch_one_shot` function directly with `pytest.raises(SystemExit)`.
Actually, a better approach: I can override `FakeGitHub.get_pr_for_branch` to always return a PRDetails. But that would require subclassing. Let me look at this differently - I can make the test use the CLI runner and check the `--slug` flag. The branch name from `generate_planned_pr_branch_name("fix-config-import", time.now(), ...)` will be deterministic if I control the time. And the context builder uses `FakeTime` by default.
Let me check what time `FakeTime` returns:

session.assistant:
So with slug "fix-config-import" and default fake time `2024-01-15 14:30:00`, the branch name would be `plnd/fix-config-import-01-15-1430`. But I shouldn't hardcode that — let me rethink the PR test.
A better approach: Instead of trying to predict the branch name, I can just test the `dispatch_one_shot` function directly using `pytest.raises(SystemExit)`. But actually, the problem is I need to configure `prs_by_branch` with a key matching the generated branch name. Since `FakeGitHub.get_pr_for_branch` does a dict lookup by exact branch name, I need to predict the name.
Given the FakeTime default (`2024-01-15 14:30:00`), and `generate_planned_pr_branch_name("fix-config-import", datetime(2024, 1, 15, 14, 30, 0), objective_id=None)`, the branch should be `plnd/fix-config-import-01-15-1430`.
Let me verify:

"Fix Auth Bug", datetime(2024, 1, 15, 14, 30), objective_id=None 845→ ... ) 846→ "plnd/fix-auth-bug-01-15-1430" 847→ >>> generate_planned_pr_branch_name(

1→"""Shared dispatch logic for one-shot autonomous execution. 2→ 3→Extracts the branch-create/push/PR/workflow-dispatch sequence into reusable 4→pieces so both `erk one-shot` and `erk objective plan --one-shot` can 5→dispatch tasks through the same CI workflow. 6→""" 7→ 8→import logging 9→import time 10→from dataclasses import dataclass 11→from datetime import UTC 12→ 13→import click 14→ 15→from erk.cli.commands.pr.metadata_helpers import write_dispatch_metadata 16→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 17→from erk.core.branch_slug_generator import generate_branch_slug 18→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel, RepoContext 19→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptExecutor 20→from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.types import BranchAlreadyExists 21→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError 22→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 24→ create_submission_queued_block, 25→ render_erk_issue_event, 26→) 27→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import format_plan_header_body 28→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import construct_workflow_run_url 29→from erk_shared.gateway.github.pr_footer import build_pr_body_footer 30→from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time
... [15 lines omitted] ...

222→ 223→ # Create branch from trunk 224→ current_step = "Creating branch" 225→ user_output("Creating branch...") 226→ branch_result = ctx.git.branch.create_branch(repo.root, branch_name, trunk, force=False) 227→ if isinstance(branch_result, BranchAlreadyExists): 228→ user_output( 229→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") 230→ + f"Branch '{branch_name}' already exists locally. " 231→ + "Run 'erk delete <branch>' to clean up, then retry." 232→ ) 233→ raise SystemExit(1) 234→ user_output(click.style(" \u2713 Branch created", dim=True)) 235→ 236→ # Write prompt to .erk/impl-context/prompt.md directly on the branch (no checkout) 237→ current_step = "Committing prompt file" 238→ user_output("Committing prompt file...") 239→ ctx.git.commit.commit_files_to_branch( 240→ repo.root, 241→ branch=branch_name, 242→ files={".erk/impl-context/prompt.md": params.prompt + "\n"}, 243→ message=f"One-shot: {params.prompt[:60]}", 244→ ) 245→ user_output(click.style(" \u2713 Committed", dim=True)) 246→ 247→ # Push to remote 248→ current_step = "Pushing to remote" 249→ user_output("Pushing to remote...") 250→ push_start = time.monotonic() 251→ push_result = ctx.git.remote.push_to_remote(
... [25 lines omitted] ...
